title: Unit 5 Learn Greek Grammar Masterclass: The Accusative Case and Prepositions

script.text: Welcome... to the Learn Greek Grammar Masterclass for Unit 5: The Accusative Case and Prepositions. You've learned how to name things... and how to make them act with verbs. But now... we need to know WHO the action is happening to. This is the world of the Accusative Case. It is the destination... the target. I'm William, and with me as always is Athina. Athina... what is the primary role of the Accusative?

The Accusative is the 'Direct Object' case. If I do something to a noun... that noun must be in the Accusative. We touched on this in Unit 2... but today we go deep. Let's look at the masculine nouns again. They are the ones that change most.
The 'S' at the end drops away... and the article grows an 'N'.
The noun stays the same... but the article changes to

Neuters are easy! They never change between subject and object. But the Accusative isn't just for direct objects. It’s also the 'Case of Prepositions'. Athina... explain what happens when we use words like 'in', 'to', or 'at'.
meaning in, to, or at... the noun following it MUST be in the Accusative. But here is the native trick: we almost always combine
So instead of saying 'Se ton'... we say
It’s a contraction. Athina... give us some examples of moving towards a place.
Πηγαίνω στο σπίτι... Πηγαίνω στην Ελλάδα.
I'm going to the house... I'm going to Greece. Notice how
ends in 'a'. It's feminine. So we use
Now... what about other prepositions? Like 'with' or 'without'?
Με τον φίλο μου... Χωρίς το βιβλίο.

So if you see a preposition... reach for the Accusative. It's the default case for almost everything that isn't the subject. This has been a crucial lesson. Mastering the Accusative is what makes your Greek sound grammatically correct and fluid. Practice with the prepositions list on the website and try describing where you are going today. Excellent work... we'll see you in Unit 6: Adjectives and Agreement.
